design_philosophy.core_loop: Expand villages by constructing buildings and increasing population, manage worship power to cast miracles, sway villagers through assistance or intimidation, progressively take control of all villages on an island
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral alignment system - every action judges the player as good, evil, or neutral, affecting world aesthetics, interface presentation, and advisor persuasion. Temple vulnerability creates defensive pressure.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Act as a god with godly powers - manipulate the environment with a giant hand, cast miracles, control creature avatars, command followers, and battle rival gods for world domination
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Moral choices are mechanically integrated into progression and world state rather than cosmetic. The game emphasizes emergent storytelling through creature AI and villager behavior rather than predetermined narratives. Creature training and personality development creates unique playthroughs. Icon-free UI design prioritizes immersion and direct hand interaction.

difficulty.lives_system: Temple destruction results in game loss; temples are vulnerable only when god has no followers, creating strategic defensive pressure
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; islands serve as individual scenarios with goal of controlling all villages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency - player can adopt good, evil, or neutral approaches; mix moral strategies; control resource distribution and village expansion pace; use creatures and miracles strategically

progression.upgrade_system: Building blueprint unlocks and construction; wonder buildings provide specific benefits by tribe; miracle availability expands through village control; area of influence expands with village population growth
progression.unlock_mechanism: Gold scrolls trigger story events and major progression; Silver Reward Scrolls offer optional tasks granting rewards like Miracle Dispensers; blueprints unlock new buildings for construction
replayability_hooks: Moral alignment system creates fundamentally different playthroughs (good vs evil vs neutral strategies)
Creature AI personality development is influenced by player treatment, creating unique companion experiences
Multiple approaches to village persuasion (assistance vs intimidation) enable different tactical approaches
Tribal diversity with unique Wonders encourages exploring different village development paths
Multiplayer support over local network enables competitive replayability

world_building_techniques: Scroll-based narrative (Gold Scrolls for major story, Silver Scrolls for side content)
Moral alignment system that dynamically affects world aesthetics - temple appearance, interface coloration, music, and hand presentation shift based on good/evil choices
Tribal diversity and cultural differentiation through eight distinct tribes (Norse, Celtic, Japanese, etc.) with unique architectural and cultural Wonders
Creature avatar as narrative tool - AI personality mirrors player treatment, creating emergent character development
Environmental storytelling through village structures, NPC behavior, and resource availability
Two-advisor system (good and evil) providing contrasting narrative perspectives and moral guidance

boss_design.philosophy: Nemesis serves as the primary antagonist god - a rival deity attempting world takeover. Conflict is strategic (controlling villages/islands) rather than direct combat encounter. Temples act as boss-like defensive objectives.
notable_bosses: Nemesis - rival god with opposing moral alignment and competing village control strategy

faction_indicators: Moral alignment visibility - temple aesthetics and interface presentation broadcast player alignment to followers
Tribal affiliation and cultural identity through eight distinct tribes
Villager belief system - NPCs respond to player alignment and actions, creating social reputation
Worship mechanics - villagers physically gather at temples, creating visual manifestation of faith
danger_signals: Temple destruction triggers immediate game loss, signaling maximum vulnerability state
Fireballs and lightning storms used as intimidation tools create environmental danger markers
Enemy god (Nemesis) territory expansion indicates threat level
Loss of village control and follower belief represents declining power
environmental_storytelling: Temple architecture and coloration reflects god's moral alignment - bright temples for good, intimidating designs for evil
Village construction diversity and building density indicates civilization prosperity and god favor
Miracle effects and environmental manipulation create visible signs of divine intervention
Artifact ownership marked by glow color indicates territorial control and allegiance
Worshipper congregation size at temples visualizes faith strength and population devotion
